title: Ceanothus zeylanicus
taxonomy: Ceanothus zeylanicus is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It is native to Sri Lanka and is commonly known as the Ceylon lilac or the bluebottle. The plant is a shrub or small tree that grows up to 15 meters in height and has dark green leaves and clusters of blue flowers. Ceanothus zeylanicus is classified under the genus Ceanothus, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas, but have been introduced to other parts of the world. This plant is considered an important part of the ecosystem in Sri Lanka, providing food and shelter for various animals and insects.
